# Namo Labs

> Deep Tech. For Humanity.

Namo Labs is an independent deep-tech research and engineering organisation headquartered in India. We build foundational infrastructure in post-quantum cryptography, blockchain, artificial intelligence, quantum technologies, and cloud systems — for governments, enterprises, and developers worldwide.

## Core Research Areas

- **Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)**: Developing quantum-resistant algorithms, high-assurance security models, and next-generation cryptographic infrastructure for the post-quantum era.
- **Blockchain**: Layer 1 and Layer 2 research, Hyperledger enterprise frameworks, DLT interoperability, and decentralised application infrastructure.
- **Artificial Intelligence**: Intelligent systems, machine learning, computer vision, and natural language processing for real-world impact.
- **Quantum Technologies**: Quantum computing algorithms, quantum key distribution (QKD), quantum communication, and quantum-safe security protocols.
- **Cloud Technologies**: Cloud-native architectures, zero-trust cloud security, and serverless/containerised systems.

## Platforms

- **QSCL™**: Quantum-Secure Communication Layer — post-quantum secure communications platform.
- **TierraTrace™**: Blockchain-based traceability and provenance platform.
- **DAFS™**: Digital Asset Forensics Suite — launching soon.

## Consulting Services

Namo Labs provides research-backed consulting in: AI & Analytics, Blockchain, Cybersecurity, Cloud Infrastructure, Enterprise Digital Transformation, Enterprise Networking, and Sustainability Technology.

## Key Facts

- Founded by Namoj PeriaKumar
- Based in India; serving global clients

- GitHub: https://github.com/NamoLabsIndia

## Insights (Technical Articles)

- NIST's Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards: ML-KEM and ML-DSA Explained — a technical explainer on FIPS 203 (ML-KEM) and FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), their parameter sets, and sector-by-sector migration deadlines (US NSM-10 2035, EU 2030, UK NCSC 2028/2031/2035): https://namolabs.in/insights/nist-pqc-standards-ml-kem-ml-dsa-explained
